interpretation: A1-SE subject-erase moves held-out bottleneck-code L1 a SMALL, SIGNIFICANT amount (0.948->0.872, drop +0.076 CI[0.054,0.097]; ~8% rel) but BELOW the pre-registered gate (>=0.10 abs OR >=20% rel). The ~0.95 subject fingerprint is so dominant that even an EXPLICIT adversarial subject-erasure only chips ~8% off held-out separability (and in-sample L1 rises 0.94->0.98 = adapter memorizes training subjects). => INSUFFICIENT METRIC POWER: the light-adapter-on-frozen-PCA design cannot resolve a representation-level subject-diversity effect against this ceiling. STOP Phase 8 (method-limit, NOT a foundation-model null; NOT a trigger to try another adapter architecture per STOP-1).
